# Dillaaumar

*A world generated by Cartogenesis v0.11.0 from seed `cartogenesis`. Regenerate it exactly with `node src/cli.ts generate --seed cartogenesis`.*

## Overview

- **Extent:** 360 × 360 cells
- **Land / ocean:** 62.5% land, 37.5% ocean, 2 lake(s)
- **Climate & life:** dominant biome **Grassland**, 16 biomes in all
- **Peoples:** 29 regions, 25 settlements, 4 realms
- **Capital:** Bee, seat of House Rimondoo
- **Dominant power:** Dillaaumar (of 2 surviving realm(s))
- **The present year:** 826 After Reckoning

## Notable features

- **Mount Stonthuvik** — the highest peak
- **The Reontepiceo** — the greatest river
- **Lake Laussorero** — the largest inland water

- Mesiasi is an inland reach of bare grey peaks, a country of vineyards and old stone towns.
- Reuabad is an inland reach of sun-scoured dunes, its people caravan-traders and readers of stars.
- Lintenilensaumar is a coast of rolling grassland, a country of vineyards and old stone towns, seat of Teollacaullabe.

## Notable figures

- **Eneve, the Heretic** — Eneve was exiled from Bee for denying the old gods.
- **Cauneo, the Scholar** — Cauneo mapped the waters of Laussorero.
- **Cidau, the Architect** — Cidau raised the great road between Bee and Miti.
- **Mintereriatemar, the Explorer** — Mintereriatemar charted the far coasts and traced the Reontepiceo to its source.

## Faiths

### the Way of Nicaudi — Nicaudi, god of the Sun

> Each dawn Nicaudi drives the sun over Stonthuvik; the people of Mesiasi greet it with fire.

Followed across 19 region(s).

### the Rite of Umerreabad — Umerreabad, god of the Sea

> In the first age the waters covered all, until Umerreabad drew up the land and set the Reontepiceo running to the sea.

Followed across 7 region(s).

### the Faith of Dogrbavik — Dogrbavik, god of the Harvest

> Dogrbavik scattered the first seed across Grobovik; the golden fields were the gift, and the tithe the price.

Followed across 2 region(s).

### the Rite of Aeansiia — Aeansiia, god of the Storm

> Aeansiia rides the storms down the Reontepiceo, and Elaeeleowynly both fears and blesses the rain.

Followed across 1 region(s).

## Resources & trade

- **Major exports:** Livestock, Horses, Grain, Wine, Fish
- **Wealthiest settlement:** Iabauti
- **Trade hubs:** Bee, Miti, Iabauti, Staaullu
- **Deposits:** Iron ×34, Copper ×26, Gold ×11, Gems ×7, Stone ×79, Coal ×34, Timber ×10, Grain ×51, Livestock ×141, Fish ×11, Furs ×9, Spices ×2, Salt ×32, Horses ×39, Wine ×23

## Legends of the founding age

- **72 After Reckoning** — The city of Bee was founded on a sheltered harbour, destined to become the seat of power.
- **94 After Reckoning** — The city of Miti was founded amid good country.
- **100 After Reckoning** — Bee rose to rule its hinterland, and the realm of Dillaaumar was proclaimed.
- **116 After Reckoning** — The city of Iabauti was founded on a sheltered harbour.
- **120 After Reckoning** — Miti rose to rule its hinterland, and the realm of Nomorindopiavento was proclaimed.
- **128 After Reckoning** — The city of Siamordeco was founded on a sheltered harbour.
- **137 After Reckoning** — Iabauti rose to rule its hinterland, and the realm of Nopa was proclaimed.
- **160 After Reckoning** — Siamordeco rose to rule its hinterland, and the realm of Neivia was proclaimed.
- **450 After Reckoning** — An academy founded on the shores of Laussorero gathered the age's finest cartographers.
- **612 After Reckoning** — Stonthuvik woke in fire and ash; a hard winter followed, and harvests failed across the land.
- **750 After Reckoning** — A great storm-tide flooded Bee; the lower quarters were lost to the sea.
- **802 After Reckoning** — Under a long peace, Bee flourished; its libraries and roads became the wonder of the world.
